attempts.approach: run proc for yesterday (20260621); compare common date
rebuild 20260619 slice directly via proc; recompare

park_reason: Point-in-time unrealized-PnL fact (grain CID + DateModified). The proc recomputes each day's open-position/price snapshot from upstream, but the replicated daily_snapshot only holds the LATEST day's point-in-time state, so the proc cannot reproduce any historical DateModified slice: re-running for 20260619 produced 2,788,950 rows vs gold 2,790,998 (worse than the pre-existing stale slice's 2,790,988), with large PnL/NOP/Notional diffs. Gold also runs ~2 days ahead of the replicated source (gold max 20260621 vs mig max 20260619), so no date exists where the migration can freshly+correctly produce the slice AND gold holds the matching settled value. NOT a transpilation/logic defect and NOT seedable from gold without faking parity. Needs full historical point-in-time source replication to validate. Parked per 5-pass guardrail (root cause is environmental; further identical-outcome passes skipped).
note: PARKED: environmental partial-replication limit on point-in-time source; proc cannot reproduce historical slices.
